page: UpCloud 80xCPU-512GB is a general-purpose server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7543 processor with 80 physical cores and 80 vCPUs, operating at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing 6.4 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of a 2048 GB SSD. The CPU allocation is shared, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU testing, bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, and PassMark CPU metrics, including encryption and physics tests. Single-core performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests. Memory bandwidth is also highly capable, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 5364.31 GB/sec and large block uncached write bandwidth at 112.07 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations both perform in the top 25%, indicating strong transactional capabilities.

While multi-threaded and multi-core operations are highly efficient, single-threaded tasks such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression fall into the average tier. Additionally, LLM text generation on medium models is weak, producing only 6.53 tokens per second, which highlights a clear tradeoff for complex AI generation tasks without dedicated GPU acceleration. Given the high physical core count, balanced memory footprint, and bundled 2 TB SSD, this server is well-suited for multi-threaded compilation, database hosting, Redis caching layers, and parallelized data processing workloads.
description: The UpCloud 80xCPU-512GB is a general-purpose KVM-based server configured with an AMD EPYC 7543 processor. It delivers 80 physical cores and 80 vCPUs at 2.0 GHz, paired with 512.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 2048 GB SSD. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% for multi-threaded decompression and PassMark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth and database operations also show strong performance in the top 25%. However, single-threaded tasks and LLM text generation represent performance tradeoffs, with medium model text generation ranking in the bottom 25%. This hardware profile is highly efficient for resource-dense, multi-threaded workloads, making it suitable for database hosting, software compilation, and parallel data processing.
og_description: UpCloud 80xCPU-512GB is a general-purpose KVM server featuring 80 AMD EPYC cores, 512 GB RAM, and a 2 TB SSD, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for databases and parallel processing.
meta_description: UpCloud 80xCPU-512GB is a KVM server with 80 AMD EPYC cores, 512 GB RAM, and 2 TB SSD, optimized for multi-threaded database and compute workloads.
tagline: A high-density, multi-core x86_64 platform featuring 512 GB RAM and bundled SSD storage for demanding parallel computing and database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7543 processor with 80 physical cores and 80 vCPUs.
Equipped with 512 GB of RAM and a bundled 2048 GB SSD.
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and multi-threaded decompression capabilities.
Strong memory bandwidth and database transaction performance.
Tradeoffs include average single-threaded performance and weak LLM text generation.
Best suited for database hosting, Redis caching, and software compilation.
